title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 18)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington's 91 Rice St. ranch sold for $460,000; 704 sq ft, built in 1951 on a 7,235 sq ft lot.
Boston's highest sale: 89 Beacon St. #2, a condo row-middle, with 5,703 sq ft and 8 baths, sold for $17M.
Arlington's 5 Lincoln St. Colonial, built in 1890 with 3,292 sq ft, sold for $2.5 million, features 4 beds, 4 baths.

title: 3 things the Bruins must fix if they want to punch their ticket to the playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' power play has struggled post-Olympic break, dropping from third to 28th in the NHL rankings.
Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm have failed to produce offensively on the third line since the Olympic break.
Defensive pairings, particularly Andrew Peeke and Nikita Zadorov, have been outscored 4-2 since returning from the Olympic break.

title: 5 Red Sox takeaways as Wilyer Abreu and Eduardo Rodriguez lift Venezuela to World Baseball Classic title
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Venezuela won its first World Baseball Classic title, defeating Team USA 3-2 in the final.
Red Sox's Wilyer Abreu hit a crucial home run, while Eduardo Rodriguez pitched 4.1 scoreless innings for Venezuela.
Red Sox's Anthony and Whitlock struggled in the final, with Whitlock allowing the winning run in the ninth inning.

title: This Ashland mom is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children with complex medical needs and their parents
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mengyuan Flanagan from Ashland is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Michael Lisnow Respite Center.
The Michael Lisnow Respite Center supports children with complex medical needs and provides relief for their families.
Flanagan started running for mental health benefits and now uses it to help families facing rare diseases.

title: Western Mass. couple found guilty of manslaughter in death of foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker were found guilty of manslaughter in the death of their 10-month-old foster child.
The child died from untreated medical conditions, including a strep infection, bronchopneumonia, and empyema, prosecutors stated.
The couple faces sentencing on March 19, with potential prison terms up to 20 years for manslaughter and two-and-a-half years for endangerment.

title: Major water main break in Jackson Square floods streets, causes detours
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A major water main broke in Jackson Square, Jamaica Plain, causing extensive flooding and disrupting traffic.
Boston police rerouted traffic, and MBTA detoured several bus routes; Orange Line service was unaffected.
Repair crews shut the water halfway by 5:40 p.m.; nearby businesses, including daycares, were evacuated.

title: Why Jaylen Waddle’s trade to Broncos is bad news for the Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Waddle was traded from the Miami Dolphins to the Denver Broncos for a first-round pick and two other draft selections.
The New England Patriots may face challenges acquiring A.J. Brown, as Denver's trade for Waddle sets a high market price.
The Patriots might need to offer a first-round pick to secure Brown, given current trading values for top wide receivers.

title: 2 charged after car hits pedestrians outside of downtown Boston nightclub
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Darius Boyce, 45, and Maria Moraes, 25, charged after a Jeep hit three pedestrians outside ICON nightclub in Boston.
Boyce faces multiple charges, including felony assault and firearm violations; Moraes charged with interference and disorderly conduct.
Incident occurred on Feb. 28; victims identified suspects, leading to their arrest shortly after the event.

title: Former President Biden makes surprise St. Patrick’s Day appearance in Boston
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former President Joe Biden made an unscheduled appearance at the Irish American Partnership's St. Patrick's Day Breakfast in Boston.
Biden spoke about his Irish ancestors and compared their immigration experiences to modern challenges under President Trump's policies.
Governor Maura Healey highlighted Boston's strong Irish connections and called for reflection on immigrant experiences in the U.S.

title: 2 Boston College students injured in deck collapse during off-campus party
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Boston College students were injured in a deck collapse at an off-campus party in Brighton.
The deck railing broke due to rotting wood, causing the students to fall from the second floor.
Authorities noted rotting wood and concealed damage; the investigation into the incident is ongoing.
